name: Bayesian Goal-Inference Model with Executive Capacity Prior
description: A probabilistic Bayesian framework where the agent maintains a posterior belief over whether to pursue a 'hedonic eating' goal vs. a 'dietary restraint' goal. Executive function capacity (inhibitory control and working memory) is encoded as a prior favoring restraint, while sensory evidence (food cues and hunger) updates beliefs toward eating via likelihood functions. The posterior is combined with learned Q-values for each goal to produce value-modulated action probabilities via softmax selection. This creates a principled integration of top-down executive priors with bottom-up sensory evidence, where EF depletion shifts the prior toward eating. Q-value learning allows the agent to adapt its preferences based on experienced rewards.

variables.name: posterior_eat_probability
variables.description: Agent's posterior belief that eating is the correct goal/action on this step

variables.name: prior_restraint
variables.description: Executive-control-derived prior probability favoring dietary restraint

variables.name: likelihood_eat
variables.description: Likelihood of current sensory evidence given eating goal (food cues + hunger)

variables.name: likelihood_restrain
variables.description: Likelihood of current state given restraint goal (1 - L_eat)

variables.name: inhibitory_control
variables.description: Current inhibitory control capacity; shapes prior toward restraint

variables.name: working_memory
variables.description: Current working memory availability; supports goal maintenance in prior

variables.name: hunger
variables.description: Homeostatic hunger signal feeding into likelihood of eating goal

variables.name: food_cue_salience
variables.description: Perceived food-cue salience from nearest/most palatable visible food

variables.name: q_value_eat
variables.description: Learned expected reward from eating actions

variables.name: q_value_restrain
variables.description: Learned expected reward from restraint actions

variables.name: utility_eat
variables.description: Value-weighted utility of eating: posterior * Q_eat

parameters.name: softmax_temperature
parameters.source: Controls action stochasticity in softmax selection

parameters.name: ic_weight_in_prior
parameters.source: Strong effect of IC on prior (Wiley / Eating Disorders, 2023; CORE, 2016)

parameters.name: ic_recovery_rate
parameters.source: Recovery rate when no food cues present

parameters.name: wm_load_per_food_type
parameters.source: Each distinct food type in view loads working memory (PMC, 2017)

parameters.name: wm_recovery_rate
parameters.source: WM recovers when no food visible

parameters.name: internal_restraint_reward_no_hunger
parameters.source: Internal reward for restraint when not hungry

parameters.name: internal_restraint_penalty_hungry
parameters.source: Internal penalty for restraint when very hungry

rules.description: Compute food-cue salience S from the most salient visible food item (highest palatability / distance ratio)
Compute prior toward restraint using logistic sigmoid of IC and WM
rules.pseudocode:
z = lambda_IC * IC + lambda_WM * WM
pi_restrain = 1.0 / (1.0 + exp(-z))
pi_eat = 1.0 - pi_restrain

rules.description: Compute likelihood of sensory evidence given eating goal using logistic sigmoid of hunger and salience
rules.pseudocode:
z = lambda_hunger * H + lambda_cue * S
L_eat = 1.0 / (1.0 + exp(-z))
L_restrain = 1.0 - L_eat

rules.description: Compute posterior probability of eating goal via Bayes' rule
rules.pseudocode:
numerator = pi_eat * L_eat
denominator = pi_eat * L_eat + pi_restrain * L_restrain
p_eat = numerator / denominator if denominator > 0 else 0.5

rules.description: Compute value-modulated utilities by combining posterior with learned Q-values
rules.pseudocode:
U_eat = p_eat * Q_eat
U_restrain = (1.0 - p_eat) * Q_restrain

rules.description: Softmax action selection based on utilities
rules.pseudocode:
exp_eat = exp(U_eat / tau)
exp_restrain = exp(U_restrain / tau)
P_choose_eat = exp_eat / (exp_eat + exp_restrain)
u = random.uniform(0, 1)
goal = 'eat' if u < P_choose_eat else 'restrain'

rules.description: Q-value update for eating after receiving environment reward
Q-value update for restraint using internal reward signal
Inhibitory control depletion when food cues present, recovery when absent
Working memory dynamics: loaded by number of distinct food items visible, recovers when none visible
Hunger increases each step, decreases when agent eats

decision_logic.description: Bayesian inference pipeline: (1) compute executive-control prior favoring restraint via logistic sigmoid of IC+WM, (2) compute sensory likelihood favoring eating via logistic sigmoid of hunger+salience, (3) apply Bayes' rule for posterior eat probability, (4) weight posterior by learned Q-values to get utilities, (5) softmax select between eat and restrain goals, (6) translate goal to grid action. This produces a principled probabilistic agent where EF depletion gradually shifts the prior toward eating.
pseudocode: # 1. Update internal states from last step
Update Q_eat using rule R7 (if ate last step)
Update Q_restrain using rule R8 (if restrained last step)
Compute S using rule R1 (max palatability / (1 + distance) over visible foods)
# 3. Compute Bayesian prior (executive control → restraint bias)
P_choose_eat = exp(U_eat / tau) / (exp(U_eat / tau) + exp(U_restrain / tau))
best_food = argmax over foods of palatability / (1 + manhattan_dist)
# 9. Store choice for next step Q-value update
Store goal as last_goal for use in next step's update rules

perception_to_variables.food_sources: perception.resources.food  # list of {x, y, type, palatability}
perception_to_variables.food_palatability: each food item's .palatability property (float 0.1–1.0); maps to hedonic value/salience

env_mapping.reward_source: eat action → ConsumeEffect reward (1.0) feeds into Q_eat update; restraint reward is computed internally based on hunger level for Q_restrain update

expected_behaviors.description: As inhibitory control depletes from food exposure, the prior shifts from restraint toward eating, increasing p_eat
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: Start with IC=0.75 near food → after 12 steps of food exposure, assert IC < 0.5 and p_eat has increased from initial 0.3 toward 0.5+

expected_behaviors.description: High hunger and high food-cue salience produce strong likelihood toward eating, overriding moderate executive prior
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: Set H=0.9, S=0.8, IC=0.6, WM=0.6 → compute p_eat → assert p_eat > 0.6

expected_behaviors.description: Strong executive control (high IC and WM) maintains restraint even with moderate food cues
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: Set IC=0.95, WM=0.9, H=0.3, S=0.5 → compute p_eat → assert p_eat < 0.3

expected_behaviors.description: Q-values adapt over time: repeated eating with reward increases Q_eat, making eat choices more likely; restraint when not hungry increases Q_restrain
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: Run 50 steps where agent eats frequently with reward=1.0 → assert Q_eat > Q_restrain; then run 50 steps where agent restrains with H<0.4 → assert Q_restrain increases

expected_behaviors.description: Working memory degrades when many food items are visible, weakening the restraint prior
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: Set 5 food items all within close range → assert WM decreases over steps → assert pi_restrain decreases

expected_behaviors.description: Softmax temperature controls exploration: low tau produces more deterministic choices aligned with higher utility
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: Set tau=0.05 with U_eat > U_restrain → run 100 samples → assert >95% choose eat; set tau=1.0 → assert more balanced distribution

references: ScienceDirect / Current Opinion in Behavioral Sciences (2022) — The brain bases of regulation of eating behaviors
APA PsycNet (2023) — The brain bases of regulation of eating behaviors
CORE / Frontiers in Psychology (2016) — Impulsivity and Inhibitory Control Deficits Are Associated with Obesity
ScienceDirect / Appetite (2017) — Executive functions and the self-regulation of eating behavior: A review
Springer / Reviews in Endocrine and Metabolic Disorders (2021) — Neurobiological regulation of eating behavior
Wiley / International Journal of Eating Disorders (2023) — Executive functions and eating behavior
PMC (2017) — Executive functioning and dietary intake
MDPI / Nutrients (2024) — Self-Regulation in Eating Behaviors
